---
title: "Module 04: Modeling pipeline in R"
---

- **Learning objective:** follow a full **classification** workflow in R on **Palmer Penguins** (Adelie vs Gentoo): clean the table, preprocess, pick a model, cross-validate, then read diagnostic plots.
- **Key message:** keep **preprocessing, fitting, and scoring** in one reproducible object so every model you compare is treated **fairly** — same idea as Tuesday’s slides and the [penguin classification notebook](../notebooks/penguins-classification.Rmd). Under the hood this uses the **`tidymodels`** packages (`recipe`, `workflow`, `tune_grid`, …).

## How to read the code below

You already know **`lm`** and **`glm`**. Here, R uses a slightly richer vocabulary: a **recipe** is “the list of preprocessing steps,” a **workflow** glues those steps to a **model specification**, and **`tune_grid`** tries several settings using **cross-validation**. Read the **concept slide** before each **code chunk** — the chunk names match the Tuesday deck.

[← Module library (all modules)](index.qmd)

## Canonical code (same as Tuesday slides)

{{< include ../_includes/tidymodels-pipeline.qmd >}}
